title: First Impressions of a Place
description: Share your first impressions of a city or neighborhood you visited, including what surprised you and what you liked most.
canDo: Can describe first impressions of a place visited and share what was surprising or memorable about the experience.
sampleDialogue: 京都に来たのは初めてなんですが、街が思っていたより静かで、すごく落ち着いた雰囲気でした。
practicePrompts: Tell the local what your first impression of their city was when you arrived, including one thing that was different from your expectations.
Describe a specific moment or place that left a strong impression on you and explain why it stood out.
Ask the local what they think visitors usually find most surprising about where they live.

title: Comparing Two Trips
description: Compare two travel experiences and explain which destination left a stronger impression and why.
canDo: Can compare impressions of two different places visited and recommend one to a conversation partner.
sampleDialogue: 大阪も楽しかったけど、奈良のほうが自然が多くて、ゆっくり過ごせてよかったと思います。
practicePrompts: Describe two places you have been to and compare the atmosphere or experience of each.
Explain which trip left a stronger impression on you and give two specific reasons.
Your friend asks which place they should visit first. Make a recommendation based on their interests.

title: How to Make It
description: Explain how to make a dish or craft item step by step to someone who has never done it before.
canDo: Can explain a process step by step using sequencing language and check that the listener is following.
sampleDialogue: まず材料を全部準備しておきます。次に、玉ねぎを細かく切ってから、油で炒めてください。
practicePrompts: Choose a dish or activity you know well and explain the first three steps clearly, using sequencing words like まず、次に、それから.
Your friend says they got confused at one step. Re-explain that step in a different way.
Ask your friend to repeat the full process back to you in their own words to check their understanding.

title: Something Went Wrong
titleJa: うまくいかなかったとき
description: Walk through what you did when following a procedure that did not go as planned, and troubleshoot with a more experienced person.
canDo: Can describe what steps were followed and at which point a problem occurred, and understand corrective instructions.
sampleDialogue: レシピどおりにやったつもりなんですが、焼いている途中で焦げてしまって……どこが間違っていたと思いますか。
practicePrompts: Explain what you tried to do and describe each step you took before the problem occurred.
Tell the helper exactly where things went wrong and what the result looked like.
Listen to the helper's diagnosis and ask a follow-up question to confirm you understand how to avoid the same mistake next time.

title: Taking Care of Yourself
titleJa: 自分の体を大切にする
description: Discuss daily habits for staying healthy with a friend, sharing what works for you and asking about their routine.
canDo: Can describe personal health and lifestyle habits and discuss the reasons behind them.
sampleDialogue: 最近、睡眠の質を上げるために、寝る前にスマホを見ないようにしているんです。
practicePrompts: Share one or two habits you have built into your daily routine to look after your health, and explain why you started them.
Ask your friend how they manage stress or tiredness and what lifestyle choices help them feel better.
Compare your routines and discuss one habit you might borrow from your friend.

title: When You Are Not Feeling Well
description: Explain your symptoms to a friend or pharmacist and get advice on what to do.
canDo: Can describe physical symptoms clearly and ask for practical advice about health or medication.
sampleDialogue: 昨日から頭が痛くて、少し熱っぽい感じがするんですが、何か飲んだほうがいいですか。
practicePrompts: Describe how you have been feeling since your symptoms started, including when they began and how severe they are.
Ask for a recommendation — whether to rest, take medicine, or see a doctor — and explain any relevant details like allergies.
The other person gives advice. Check that you understood correctly by repeating their suggestion back to them.

title: Talking About a Friendship
description: Describe a friend or close relationship to someone who has not met them, capturing the dynamic between you.
canDo: Can introduce and describe a personal relationship, explaining the dynamic and what you value about it.
sampleDialogue: 幼なじみなんですが、性格は全然違うんですよ。でもだからこそ、いつも新鮮で楽しいんです。
practicePrompts: Introduce a close friend or family member to your new acquaintance: describe their personality, how you met, and how long you have known them.
Explain what you most appreciate about the relationship and what makes it work despite any differences.
Ask your new acquaintance to tell you about an important relationship in their life.

title: Navigating a Tricky Relationship
titleJa: 難しい関係について話す
description: Talk honestly about a relationship that is not always easy — a demanding colleague, a complicated family dynamic, or a drifting friendship.
canDo: Can describe the nuances and tensions in a relationship and seek or offer a sympathetic perspective.
sampleDialogue: あの人のことは嫌いじゃないんですが、一緒にいると気を使いすぎてしまって、疲れることがあるんです。
practicePrompts: Describe a relationship that has some tension or awkwardness, being careful not to be too negative — explain what is good about it too.
Share a specific recent moment that illustrated the difficulty and how you handled it.
Ask your friend for their honest perspective on the situation and what they would do in your place.

title: A Place Worth Protecting
description: Describe a natural place you care about and explain why protecting it matters to you.
canDo: Can describe a natural environment and explain why it is important to protect it, using concrete details.
sampleDialogue: 子どものころよく行っていた川なんですが、最近は水がずいぶん汚れてきていて、心配しています。
practicePrompts: Describe a natural place that is meaningful to you — a forest, river, beach, or park — and explain what it looks and feels like.
Share a change you have noticed in that environment over time and explain why it concerns you.
Ask the club member what individuals can realistically do to help protect the environment they care about.

title: Small Changes, Big Impact
titleJa: 小さな行動、大きな影響
description: Discuss everyday actions that help or harm the environment and decide which changes are realistic for you to make.
canDo: Can discuss the environmental impact of daily habits and commit to realistic personal changes.
sampleDialogue: マイバッグを持ち歩くようにしたんですが、一人ではどれだけ効果があるのかわからなくて……。
practicePrompts: Share two or three things you already do — or recently started doing — to reduce your environmental impact.
Your friend mentions a habit you had not thought about. Discuss whether you could realistically adopt it and what the barriers might be.
Agree on one specific change you will both try to make over the next month, and explain why you chose that one.

title: That Is Just How We Do It
titleJa: うちではこうするんです
description: Explain a custom or tradition from your culture to someone from a different background, and learn about one of theirs in return.
canDo: Can explain a cultural custom or tradition and ask questions about a different culture's practices with genuine curiosity.
participants: Person from a different cultural background
sampleDialogue: 私の国では誕生日に家族全員が集まって食事をするのが普通なんですが、日本ではどうですか。
practicePrompts: Describe a custom from your own culture — a celebration, greeting style, or everyday etiquette — and explain its background or meaning.
Ask your partner about a similar occasion in their culture and listen for one key difference.
Discuss a moment when you did not know the local custom and how you handled it.

title: Culture Shock Moments
titleJa: カルチャーショックの瞬間
description: Share a moment when a cultural difference surprised or confused you, and talk about what you learned from it.
canDo: Can narrate a cross-cultural misunderstanding or surprise and reflect on what the experience taught them.
sampleDialogue: 日本に来た最初の週、電車の中でみんなが静かにしていて、話していいのかどうか全然わからなかったんです。
practicePrompts: Tell your friend about a moment when you were surprised or confused by a cultural norm you did not expect.
Explain how you reacted in the moment and what you did to figure out what the expected behavior was.
Ask your friend about a similar experience they had abroad and what they took away from it.

title: I Think We Should Talk About This
titleJa: これについて話し合いたい
description: Raise an opinion on a topic you feel strongly about and invite a respectful exchange of views.
canDo: Can clearly express a personal opinion on a topic and invite and respond to a differing view politely.
sampleDialogue: 最近、若者が選挙に行かないことが多いと聞きますが、どう思いますか。私はもっと関心を持つべきだと感じています。
practicePrompts: State your opinion on a topic that matters to you — education, technology, the environment, or society — and give two reasons why you hold that view.
Your partner expresses a different opinion. Show that you heard them by restating their point, then explain where you agree or differ.
Try to reach at least one point of common ground before closing the conversation.

title: Changing My Mind
description: Describe a topic on which your opinion shifted over time and explain what caused you to reconsider.
canDo: Can explain how and why a personal opinion changed, and discuss what it takes to reconsider a held belief.
sampleDialogue: 以前はSNSは時間の無駄だと思っていたんですが、使い方によっては本当に役に立つと感じるようになりました。
practicePrompts: Share an opinion you used to hold firmly and explain what it was based on.
Describe the experience, conversation, or information that made you start to think differently.
Ask your acquaintance if there is a topic on which their views have evolved, and discuss what makes people willing — or reluctant — to change their minds.
